summary: Validated backend-node conversion readiness by running syntax checks, starting Node server from src/server.js, and executing pytest coverage for health, user auth/profile, booking creation, and admin demo-logins/analytics endpoints.

critical.issue: Default admin credentials are hardcoded in backend-node/src/config.js (admin@dialforhelp.com / Admin@123) instead of environment variables.

minor.issue: config.js uses fallback defaults for PORT/CORS/default admin values; this conflicts with fail-fast env-only configuration guidance.

action_items: Move default admin email/password to env variables and remove hardcoded credential fallback.
Adopt strict env fail-fast behavior for config values where defaults are currently used.
critical_code_review_comments: backend-node/src/app.js is 946 lines (>700 guideline) and should be split into focused route/service modules for maintainability and safer debugging.
Hardcoded admin credentials in source code are a security risk and can leak privileged access in non-demo deployments.
seed_data_creation: No explicit seed script executed; tests created isolated TEST-style records through API calls.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Node backend validated locally on port 8011 via pytest fixture that starts src/server.js. Endpoints verified: GET /api/, POST /api/users/register, POST /api/users/login, GET /api/users/profile, POST /api/bookings, POST /api/admin/login, GET /api/admin/demo-logins, GET /api/admin/analytics.
rca of the issue: RCA (credential risk): backend-node/src/config.js defines defaultAdminEmail/defaultAdminPassword as constants. If env overrides are absent in any environment, the known credential pair remains valid, enabling predictable privileged access. Mitigation: source these values only from env vars, fail startup if missing, and rotate any previously used defaults.
